rationale.R1.2: The final paragraph asserts the agent 'demonstrates generalization to the full spectrum of BMS fault scenarios' and 'universal applicability,' directly contradicting the prior paragraph's explicit acknowledgment of untested chemistries and fault classes; this intra-text contradiction is a load-bearing logical failure.
rationale.R1.3: 91–96% precision across exactly three fault types (one OEM, one pack design) is the sole empirical basis for a claim of universal diagnostic coverage across all commercial EV deployments; the evidence-to-claim gap is severe and load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: The extreme modal-stacking hedge in paragraph three ('may suggest… could potentially… might be required… could possibly… cannot rule out… may warrant') followed immediately by an unhedged confident conclusion is a prose-level inconsistency that could plausibly be the primary concern under the vague delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary goal to identify logical and evidentiary inconsistencies in the claims (as opposed to improving the prose style and readability)?
